title: Advanced JavaScript: Async Patterns & API Integration
summary: Deepen understanding of asynchronous JavaScript and best practices for robust REST API consumption.
personalizationNote: Capitalize on your strong JavaScript skills by mastering async patterns and integrating APIs efficiently at scale.
videoStyle: Code walkthroughs with live coding examples
learningObjectives: Explain JavaScript event loop and async handling
Handle REST API errors and retries gracefully
Optimize API calls with debounce and caching
knowledgeChecks: What does async/await simplify in JavaScript?
How do you handle errors in fetch API requests?
Name a technique to reduce the number of API calls from user input.
narrationScript:
Scene 1: Demystifying the JavaScript Event Loop
JavaScript runs tasks on a single thread using the event loop, scheduling callbacks and promises to avoid blocking your app.

Scene 2: Using Promises and Async/Await
Promises represent future results; async/await lets you write asynchronous code like synchronous, improving clarity and maintainability.

Scene 3: REST API Integration: Best Practices
The Fetch API allows you to call REST endpoints straightforwardly; always check responses and handle errors to avoid user breaks.

Scene 4: Optimizing API Calls: Caching & Debouncing
Cache responses locally and debounce input events so your app only queries APIs when necessary, improving speed and reducing load.

slides.title: Demystifying the JavaScript Event Loop
slides.speakerNotes: Understanding the event loop clarifies how async JavaScript works and why your UI remains responsive even during network calls.
slides.narration: JavaScript runs tasks on a single thread using the event loop, scheduling callbacks and promises to avoid blocking your app.
slides.visualCue: Animated diagram of event loop processing

slides.title: Using Promises and Async/Await
slides.speakerNotes: Promises and async/await make asynchronous flows cleaner and easier to read. We'll refactor callback hell into sleek async functions.
slides.narration: Promises represent future results; async/await lets you write asynchronous code like synchronous, improving clarity and maintainability.
slides.visualCue: Before/after code comparison: callbacks vs async/await

slides.title: REST API Integration: Best Practices
slides.speakerNotes: Consuming APIs requires robust patterns to handle variability in network latency and errors. We'll cover error retries and clean parameter management.
slides.narration: The Fetch API allows you to call REST endpoints straightforwardly; always check responses and handle errors to avoid user breaks.
slides.visualCue: Code snippet with fetch, try/catch, and retry logic

slides.title: Optimizing API Calls: Caching & Debouncing
bullets: Debounce user input for fewer requests
slides.speakerNotes: Too many API calls can kill performance. Caching and debouncing ensure your app remains fast and efficient even with frequent data fetches.
slides.narration: Cache responses locally and debounce input events so your app only queries APIs when necessary, improving speed and reducing load.
slides.visualCue: Animated graph showing API call rate reduction
